architecture.summary: BAR-SQL is a unified NL2SQL training framework that generates SQL queries from natural language while detecting and abstaining on ambiguous or unanswerable queries. The system combines Seed Mutation data synthesis to create representative training data (including boundary cases), Knowledge-Grounded Reasoning Synthesis to produce schema-anchored Chain-of-Thought reasoning, and a two-stage training pipeline: Supervised Fine-Tuning followed by Reinforcement Learning via Group Relative Policy Optimization. A Task-Conditioned Hybrid Reward mechanism jointly scores both SQL execution accuracy (via Abstract Syntax Tree analysis and dense result matching) and semantic precision in abstention decisions. Information flows from natural language input through reasoning traces anchored in schema metadata, to SQL generation and abstention decisions scored by the hybrid reward.
trained_components: Group Relative Policy Optimization reward optimization

biblio.title: Boundary-Aware NL2SQL: Integrating Reliability through Hybrid Reward and Data Synthesis

claims.statement: BAR-SQL achieves 91.48% average accuracy on Ent-SQL-Bench
BAR-SQL outperforms leading proprietary models, including Claude 4.5 Sonnet and GPT-5, in both SQL generation quality and boundary-aware abstention capability
Seed Mutation data synthesis constructs a representative enterprise corpus explicitly encompassing multi-step analytical queries alongside boundary cases including ambiguity and schema limitations
Knowledge-Grounded Reasoning Synthesis produces Chain-of-Thought traces explicitly anchored in schema metadata and business rules

Task-Conditioned Hybrid Reward mechanism simultaneously optimizes SQL execution accuracy (leveraging Abstract Syntax Tree analysis and dense result matching) and semantic precision in abstention responses
The model is trained through a two-stage process: Supervised Fine-Tuning followed by Reinforcement Learning via Group Relative Policy Optimization
